description_long: This object is a detailed model of a slipper lobster, specifically resembling the genus Scyllaridae, commonly found in marine environments. Its body is robust and flattened, with a mottled brown and tan coloration that provides camouflage against rocky or sandy substrates. The cephalothorax is broad and shield-like, with two large, paddle-shaped antennae that are flattened and extend forward, giving the lobster its characteristic 'slipper' appearance. The abdomen is segmented and tapers toward the tail, with several pairs of walking legs visible beneath the body. The surface texture is rough and bumpy, with small spines and tubercles distributed across the carapace. No large claws are present, and the eyes are small, set close together on the anterior margin of the head.
description: A robust, mottled brown slipper lobster with a broad, flattened body, paddle-like antennae, and no large claws. Its rough, spiny surface and segmented abdomen are clearly visible.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the slipper lobster, highlighting the broad cephalothorax, segmented abdomen, and paddle-shaped antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizing the wide, shield-like carapace, mottled coloration, and symmetrical body shape.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the curvature of the body and the arrangement of the walking legs.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the flattened head, paddle-like antennae, and the close-set eyes at the front margin.
